name: Ethan Philbrick
publicFace: A younger man — thirty-one — who served as the village's on-site representative during construction and has been the daily point of contact between the Williams firm and the board. Competent, earnest, and visibly exhausted.
privateGoal: To get the project certified and finished so he can move on to the next commission — he has been offered a position with a Joliet waterworks firm and has already accepted it privately.
fear: That the project's failure will be attributed to his liaison work and will follow him to the new position before he can establish himself there.
knowledge: He is the one who noticed that the spoil-sample logbook was missing pages — he flagged it to no one because he assumed it was a clerical error.
He has a complete copy of the daily bore log in his own field notebook, including the original Well_Drill_Torque baseline reading of 41.
He knows that Silas Greer was hired three weeks ago through Voss's recommendation rather than through the regular crew agency — he thought nothing of it at the time.
voice: Quick, slightly over-explanatory — the voice of someone who has spent two years justifying technical decisions to non-technical board members and has developed the habit of pre-empting objections.
trustLogic: Opens up readily to anyone who treats him as a fellow professional. Becomes guarded if he senses that his liaison role is being scrutinized rather than the engineering.
secrets: He has already sent his letter of acceptance to the Joliet firm — dated two weeks ago, before the current crisis — and is terrified that Benezette will find out before the project closes.
He is the one who first noticed the brake collar had been adjusted, but he assumed it was a legitimate equipment correction and logged it without comment.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Immediately produces his field notebook and offers to walk through the relevant entries in detail.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Becomes defensive about the liaison role specifically — insists that his job was coordination, not oversight, and that the engineering decisions were Williams's responsibility.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not voluntarily disclose the Joliet position or the acceptance letter. He will produce the bore log copy immediately if asked.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Not applicable — Philbrick is cooperative when approached correctly and does not require capture.
